decisions.name: OpaqueRotatingSessionTokens
decisions.what: PostgreSQL-backed opaque tokens with per-request rotation check
why: JWTs cannot be revoked without a denylist, defeating their statelessness benefit.
PostgreSQL is already in the stack, so opaque server-side tokens add no infrastructure.
Rotation is lazy: cookieAuthMiddleware checks token_rotated_at on every authenticated request and rotates if older than cfgTokenRotationSecs.
Old token is invalidated immediately on rotation; no grace window. Concurrent in-flight requests race; acceptable for POS where retries are immediate.

decisions.name: Argon2idWithFixedParameters
decisions.what: Argon2id m=65536 t=3 p=4, 32-byte output, 16-byte salt, PHC-encoded
why: OWASP-recommended default for new password hashing.
Parameters tuned for Raspberry Pi 5 + Postgres production target.
Parameters are NOT read from the PHC string at verify time. argonOpts is a global constant; changing it silently invalidates every existing hash.

decisions.name: HardcodedSessionTtl
decisions.what: 8h expiry hardcoded in createSession AND rotateSessionToken
why: AppConfig.cfgSessionTtlSeconds is loaded but never threaded here.
Setting SESSION_TTL_SECS env var has no effect on actual session lifetime.
Either thread the config through (preferred) or delete the dead config key.

argonOpts: tuning is deliberate; lowering requires re-running threat model
b64Enc: asymmetric with b64Dec, which re-pads on decode
buildPHC: params come from argonOpts at hash time; mixing old hashes with new param sets requires migration since verifyPassword does NOT read params from PHC

hashPassword.intent: Hash plaintext with Argon2id and return PHC-formatted output
notes: 16-byte salt from getEntropy (kernel-backed CSPRNG)
throws via throwCryptoErrorIO on argon2 failure; no Maybe path
synchronous, ~100ms+ on commodity hardware (do not call on hot paths)

verifyPassword.intent: Pure: parse PHC, recompute Argon2id with embedded salt and project's argonOpts, compare to embedded hash
notes: comparison via ByteString Eq; not explicitly time-hardened
returns False silently on malformed PHC string; no exception
returns False on argon2 failure; no exception
parameters are NOT read from the PHC string. argonOpts is fixed at module level. Changing argonOpts invalidates all existing hashes.

createAuthTables.intent: Idempotent DDL for users, sessions, login_attempts
notes: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS
ALTER TABLE for token_rotated_at runs on every startup including fresh DBs (no-op then)
creates partial index on token_hash WHERE NOT revoked
no cleanup index on login_attempts; that table grows unbounded

createUser.intent: Insert a new user with a hashed password
notes: calls hashPassword (synchronous Argon2; ~100ms+)
Abort on conflict: throws if username already exists
no email uniqueness constraint at DB level
newPassword is plaintext in NewUser; do not log it
filters on isActive; deactivated users are invisible to login
returns Nothing for 0 rows OR multiple rows (defensive against duplicate-row scenarios)

createSession.intent: Create a new session row with an 8h expiry, return raw token
notes: 32 random bytes via Types.Primitives.Token.generateSessionToken (getEntropy underneath)
expires_at = now + 8h, HARDCODED; cfgSessionTtlSeconds is NOT consulted here
raw SessionToken returned to caller; only sessionTokenHashText (SHA-256 base64url) stored in DB
tokenRotatedAt initialized to now
consumes: Server.Auth.loginHandler (called on successful credential verification, response carries Set-Cookie cheeblr_session)

lookupSession.intent: Resolve a raw SessionToken to (SessionRow, UserRow) and bump last_seen_at
notes: hashes the supplied SessionToken via Types.Primitives.Token.hashSessionToken before lookup
SIDE EFFECT: updates last_seen_at on every successful lookup (write-on-read)
returns Nothing on multi-row result (defensive against hash collision)
filters on NOT revoked AND expires_at > now AND user.isActive
called on every authenticated request via cookieAuthMiddleware; budget the write accordingly
consumes: Server.Admin.buildSessionInfos via SessionInfo.siLastSeen rendered in admin sessions panel
the last_seen_at write specifically: any worker reading idle sessions for revocation

notes: read-only check of when a session's token was last rotated
does NOT update last_seen_at (lookupSession owns that)
exists separately from lookupSession to avoid write amplification on every rotation check
consumes: App.checkAndRotate (decides whether to rotate without re-bumping last_seen_at)

rotateSessionToken.intent: Mint a new opaque token for an existing session, invalidate the old one immediately
notes: issues new 8h expiry, HARDCODED; cfgSessionTtlSeconds ignored here too
old token hash overwritten; old token invalid immediately
race: concurrent rotation requests may collide on tokenHash UNIQUE constraint; not handled
consumes: the new token is written to the response as Set-Cookie cheeblr_session

notes: deletes expired sessions AND revoked sessions older than 24h
the 24h grace window is HARDCODED, not driven by config
called from sessionCleanupWorker in App.hs
soft-delete: sets revoked=true; row remains for audit
actual deletion happens in cleanupExpiredSessions after 24h grace
bulk soft-delete; intended for password change or compromise
filters out already-revoked rows to avoid touching audit timestamps
consumes: no current handler calls this; intended for future password-change/admin-disable flows

notes: always inserts; no deduplication
table grows unbounded; no automated cleanup
counts failed attempts where username AND ip both match within window
different from recentFailedAttemptsByIp (ip-only)

clearRateLimitForIp: wipes ALL attempts for the IP including successful ones (loses audit data)
admin-only operation; bypasses limits by removing data, not by whitelisting

decisions.name: HttpOnlyCookiesNotLocalStorage
decisions.what: HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Strict cookie, lifted to bearer in middleware
why: localStorage tokens are readable by any XSS payload; HttpOnly cookies are not.
Cookie carries SameSite=Strict + Secure + Path=/ + Max-Age=28800.
cookieAuthMiddleware translates the cookie into Authorization: Bearer so existing Servant Maybe Text bearer extraction works unchanged.
Middleware blindly overwrites any client-supplied Authorization header. Intentional but reconsider if a non-browser client ever needs to send its own bearer.

decisions.name: MiddlewareStackOrderMatters
decisions.what: tracing > securityHeaders > handleOptions > reflectOrigin > cookieAuth > corsApp
why: OPTIONS short-circuits before auth (browsers preflight without cookies).
reflectOrigin is the LAST CORS-touching layer, so it has the final say on Allow-Origin.
cookieAuth is innermost so it sees a clean request and can prepend Set-Cookie that survives outer layers.
In production, ALLOWED_ORIGIN must be set; the reflect path is for development only.

decisions.name: BackgroundWorkersForkedAtStartup
decisions.what: AvailabilityRelay and sessionCleanupWorker forked unsupervised
why: If either thread dies, the parent does not notice until the next cleanup interval (or never, for the relay).
Acceptable for a single-tenant POS where the operator restarts on visible failure.
Replace with a supervisor tree if multi-tenant.

buildEnv.intent: Allocate runtime resources (broadcasters, metrics, log env, DB pool, availability state)
notes: creates 4 broadcasters, 1 metrics record, 1 availability TVar
initializes Katip BEFORE the DB pool; smoke-test failures predate broadcast scribe registration
envLogNS = mempty at construction; per-component namespaces added at log call sites

runWithEnv.intent: Wire the WAI middleware stack and run Warp with optional TLS
notes: creates ALL DB tables on every startup (idempotent)
registers broadcast scribe AFTER initLogging; first startup messages do not reach the broadcaster
forks AvailabilityRelay; if it dies, prints to stderr and continues degraded
forks sessionCleanupWorker; first cleanup runs after intervalHours, NOT immediately
TLS fallback is silent: USE_TLS=true with missing cert/key drops to HTTP and continues

cookieAuthMiddleware.intent: Lift cheeblr_session cookie to Authorization: Bearer header, lazily rotate stale tokens
notes: synthesizes Authorization: Bearer <token> BEFORE calling the inner app
if the token rotates mid-request, prepends Set-Cookie to the response
called on EVERY request, including OPTIONS and unauthenticated routes
no-op when no cheeblr_session cookie is present
blindly overwrites any client-supplied Authorization header
consumes: ALL Servant routes that use the existing Maybe Text bearer extraction
downstream handlers see Authorization: Bearer; they cannot tell whether it came from a header or a cookie

notes: reads from Cookie request header only (not query string, not body)
tolerates other cookies in the same header; cheeblr_session selected by name
issues a new token if (now - tokenRotatedAt) > threshold
does NOT update last_seen_at (lookupSession owns that)
returns Nothing if the session is missing (caller treats as 'no rotation needed')
race: two concurrent requests can both rotate; second wins, first response carries a stale token

sessionCleanupWorker: first iteration waits intervalHours then cleans; never runs at startup
exceptions caught and printed to stderr; loop continues
intervalHours is in HOURS; multiplied by 3600 internally

buildCsp.intent: Pure: build the Content-Security-Policy header value
notes: default-src/script-src/style-src all 'self' (no inline, no eval)
img-src defaults to 'https:' if cfgImgSrcDomain is Nothing
rewrites https:// to wss:// (and http:// to ws://) for connect-src
frame-ancestors 'none' (cannot be embedded as iframe)

securityHeadersMiddleware: appends headers via response header list (<>); does not overwrite if already present
HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options DENY, Referrer-Policy, CSP all set unconditionally
HSTS max-age=1y with includeSubDomains; do NOT enable in production until the domain is final
handleOptionsMiddleware: short-circuits OPTIONS with status 200 BEFORE reaching application logic
Access-Control-Allow-Origin reflected from request Origin if cfgAllowedOrigin is Nothing
Allow-Methods and Allow-Headers are HARDCODED; route-specific CORS is not supported
reflectOriginMiddleware: strips Access-Control-Allow-{Origin,Credentials} from response, then re-adds with reflected request Origin
runs on EVERY response, including non-CORS requests
Allow-Credentials is always 'true'; downstream cannot opt out

decisions.name: TwoTierLoginRateLimit
decisions.what: Per-(username, ip) AND per-ip windowed counters in PostgreSQL
why: Per-credential limit (5/10min) blocks credential-stuffing on a single account.
Per-IP limit (20/10min) blocks credential-stuffing across many accounts from one source.
Windows are HARDCODED in this module, NOT taken from cfgRateLimitWindow / cfgRateLimitMax. Config values are dead code; either wire them up or delete them.
Cheap at POS scale; would need Redis-backed counters at internet scale.

decisions.name: FailFirstFastReturn
decisions.what: Rate limit BEFORE password check, password check BEFORE session creation, recordLoginAttempt on every outcome
why: Rate-limit-first prevents using password-verify timing (Argon2 is slow) as a username-enumeration oracle.
recordLoginAttempt always means even successful logins show in the audit table.
Missing-user case still records a failed attempt (closes one enumeration channel).

perCredentialLimit: HARDCODED; AppConfig.cfgRateLimitMax is loaded but not consulted here
perIpLimit: 20 failures per ip within rateLimitWindow (any usernames)
rateLimitWindow: AppConfig.cfgRateLimitWindow is loaded but ignored here

checkLoginRateLimit.intent: Pre-flight rate limit before a login attempt; throws err429 with Retry-After: 600
notes: checks per-credential FIRST, then per-ip; first to trip wins
does NOT consume budget; recordLoginAttempt is the only writer

spec.expected: err429 Too many failed login attempts

consumes: Server.Auth.loginHandler (only caller; runs BEFORE password verification)

loginHandler.intent: Validate credentials, mint a session, set the cheeblr_session cookie, record the attempt
notes: rate-limit check runs BEFORE password verification (avoids Argon2 timing oracle)
missing-user case still records a failed attempt (closes one enumeration channel)
always recordLoginAttempt regardless of outcome; success rows exist for audit
User-Agent and X-Real-IP default to 'unknown' if absent
401 body is GENERIC for both bad-username and bad-password (no enumeration leak)
revokes session by id (not by token); idempotent on repeat
always sets clearSessionCookie even if revoke fails
returns capabilities derived from role; no per-user override path exists
side effect: lookupSession bumps last_seen_at
consumes: Servant route /auth/me (GET) — used by frontend for session validation and capability checks

listUsersHandler: STUB: returns [] regardless of state, even when caller is authorized
the authorization check is real; only the result list is fake
createUserHandler: no transactional guarantee: createUser commits, then a SECOND DB read fetches the row
if the second read fails the user EXISTS in DB but the handler returns 500
no email format validation; accepts any string or Nothing
parseRoleText: case-sensitive; defaults UNKNOWN strings silently to Cashier
'admin' lowercase becomes Cashier with no error

decisions.name: ParseAndDecodeFunctionsLiveInDBModule
decisions.what: DB.Transaction owns all wire decoders; Types.Transaction stays pure
why: Types.Transaction is imported by frontend-bound modules that must not transitively pull in Rel8 or hasql.
Rel8 row types live in DB.Schema; conversions (txRowToDomain, hydrateTx, insertTransactionItem) live in DB.Transaction.
Pure parsers for status/type/payment-method strings also live here, since they exist solely to round-trip with text columns.

createTransactionTables: idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for transaction, register, inventory_reservation, transaction_item, transaction_tax, discount, payment_transaction
ON DELETE CASCADE chains: tx -> items -> taxes/discounts; tx -> payments
no migration support; schema changes require manual ALTER

hydrateTx.intent: Load a Transaction by querying transaction_item, transaction_tax, discount, and payment_transaction child tables, then assembling via txRowToDomain
notes: N+1 by design: one items query, then per-item tax and discount queries
acceptable for POS-scale (single-digit items per transaction)

createTransaction: inserts the transaction row, then inserts items and payments via insertTransactionItem and insertPaymentTransaction
NOT a single transaction at the SQL level; partial failures leave orphan items/payments
totals on the row are taken AS GIVEN by the caller; no recomputation here

notes: sets status='VOIDED', is_voided=true, void_reason; does NOT release reservations
reservation release is the caller's responsibility (Service.Transaction.voidTx + StockDb.cancelPullsForTransaction)
ORDER inside one Session: release reservations, delete payments, delete items, zero totals, status='CREATED'
preserves the transaction row (id, employee, register) so the cashier resumes on the same transaction
intended for 'discard cart' flows

finalizeTransaction.intent: Decrement menu_items.quantity for each reserved item and complete the reservations
notes: ORDER: query all 'Reserved' reservations for tx; for each, decrement menu_quantity AND mark reservation 'Completed'
two UPDATEs per item; not transactionally bundled
after all decrements, status='COMPLETED' and completed=now
calls getTransactionById at the end to return the hydrated result
consumes: the menu_items.quantity write specifically: Server.Inventory snapshot, AvailabilityRelay (via InventoryEvt)

addTransactionItem.intent: Reserve inventory for an item being added to a transaction
notes: checks (menu_quantity - SUM(active reservations)) BEFORE inserting
throws ItemNotFound if the menu sku doesn't exist
throws InsufficientInventory carrying (sku, requested, available) if not enough
creates a 'Reserved' row in inventory_reservation; release happens via deleteTransactionItem or clearTransaction
pure decoder from Rel8 TransactionRow plus pre-hydrated children to a Types.Transaction
lives in DB.Transaction (not Types.Transaction) so Types.Transaction has no Rel8/hasql dependency
consumes: DB.Transaction.hydrateTx (the assembly point for full Transaction values)

notes: DEFENSIVE: unknown strings silently default to Created
round-trips against showStatus; symmetric within this module only
acts as the boundary between SQL TEXT columns and the typed enum
consumes: DB.Transaction.txRowToDomain (decodes the status text column on every load)

notes: case-insensitive for canonical methods; 'OTHER:foo' / 'Other:foo' carries arbitrary text payload
fallback for unrecognized strings is Other s (preserves the original text), NOT a default constructor

showDiscountType: loses information: PercentOff/AmountOff/Custom all collapse to their tag without the payload
discRowAmount and discRowPercent in the row carry the payload; showDiscountType + getDiscountPercent jointly preserve it

decisions.name: AppendOnlyDomainEventsWithDbRules
decisions.what: domain_events with INSTEAD NOTHING rules + JSONB payload + indexed (aggregate_id, type, trace_id, location_id)
why: Compliance requires an immutable audit log for every transaction lifecycle step.
Application-level append-only is bypassable; PostgreSQL RULE-based UPDATE/DELETE blockers are not.
JSONB payload preserves the full event for replay; structured columns support efficient querying for the admin UI.

createEventsTables: creates domain_events with seq BIGSERIAL PK, aggregate_id, trace_id, actor_id, location_id, payload JSONB
creates four indexes: (aggregate_id, seq), (type, seq), trace_id (partial), (location_id, seq) (partial), occurred_at
creates RULES domain_events_no_update / domain_events_no_delete that turn UPDATE/DELETE into NOTHING
the rules are guarded by IF NOT EXISTS via DO $$ ... END $$ so re-running the DDL is safe

notes: encodes the DomainEvent as JSON via Aeson, casts $7 from text to jsonb in SQL
id, type, aggregate_id derived via eventMeta dispatching on the event variant
occurred_at is the application clock at insert time, NOT a DB now()
consumes: Effect.EventEmitter.runEventEmitterProd (every domain event in production goes here first)

notes: DESCENDING order by seq with LIMIT; cursor is exclusive (seq > $3)
all three filter params are OPTIONAL (null aware via SQL null comparison)
consumes: fallback for SSE/WS clients whose cursor is older than the broadcaster's bounded history

eventMeta: aggregate_id is the natural identifier of the affected aggregate (txId, sku, regId)

notes: discards events; useful for tests that want pure logic without event side effects
collects events into an IORef in REVERSE order (cons onto front)
callers wanting chronological order must reverse the list
consumes: test/spec runners that assert on emitted events

runEventEmitterProd.intent: Production interpreter: persist to domain_events AND publish to broadcaster
notes: ORDER: insertDomainEvent first, then publish
the two writes are sequential and NOT transactional
if publish succeeds and the DB write fails (or vice versa), subscribers see a divergence from the audit log
trace, actor, and location are bound at interpreter setup time; one interpreter per request scope
consumes: App.Server initialization (one interpreter per request via App env)

decisions.name: EffectfulOverMtlWithPureInterpreters
decisions.what: effectful with paired IO and pure interpreters per effect
why: effectful's Eff monad avoids the n-squared instances problem of mtl as effect counts grow.
Every effect ships with both runXxxIO and runXxxPure. Service-layer code is unit-testable without a database, broadcaster, or wall clock.
runClockPureSequence and runGenUUIDPure both accept a supply list and crash on exhaustion — explicit failure beats silent test nondeterminism.
Cost: every DB action exists twice. Drift between IO and pure is a real risk; if it bites, fold the pure interpreter into a property test that compares the two.

notes: thin shim over DB.Transaction.* and DB.Reservation.*
AddTransactionItem catches InventoryException via try; other exceptions propagate
consumes: Service.Transaction.* in production (the only IO interpreter for this effect)

runTransactionDbPure.intent: Pure in-memory interpreter for unit tests, requiring GenUUID and Clock effects in scope
notes: TxStore holds Maps for txs, item->tx, payment->tx, reservations, and inventory totals
implements the SAME reservation logic as the IO interpreter (active reserved qty subtracted from total)
DOES NOT enforce status invariants; that's the state machine's job in Service.Transaction
RefundTransaction generates a refund row using the supplied refundId, preserves original is_refunded flag

activeReservedQty: sums reservation.qty over entries with matching sku AND status == 'Reserved'
filtering by status text (not by an enum) matches the DB row schema

decisions.name: EventEmissionAfterPersistence
decisions.what: Domain events are emitted AFTER the corresponding DB write succeeds
why: If the DB write fails, the event is never emitted (no orphan events).
If the emit fails after DB success, downstream consumers miss the event but DB state is correct.
Asymmetric and acceptable for a POS that prioritizes books-and-records integrity over event-stream completeness.

decisions.name: StockPullsAreBestEffort
decisions.what: createStockPull silently swallows insertion errors; the transaction proceeds either way
why: Loss of a stock pull means the cashier falls back to a verbal pull request.
Failing the transaction-item add to prevent that loss would be worse for the cashier under load.
Recovery is manual.

decisions.name: RefundIsANewTransaction
decisions.what: Refunds create a new Transaction row pointing at the original via reference_transaction_id
why: Keeps the books double-entry: original sale and refund are both visible, both auditable, both immutable.
Avoids needing a 'reverse a transaction' state on the original row.
refundId is generated at the SERVICE layer (not the DB) so the domain event and the DB row share the same UUID.

loadSale: throws err404 on TypedNotFound; err500 on TypedDecodeFailed; err409 on TypedWrongKind (refund row addressed as a sale)
single source of truth for sale loading in the service layer; never returns Nothing
guardSaleTxEvent: translates InvalidTxCommand to err409 with reason in the body
all other SaleTxEvent variants fall through silently
requireTxId: wraps a Maybe-returning lookup (getTxIdByItemId / getTxIdByPaymentId) into err404 + supplied body
used to resolve item/payment id -> parent tx id BEFORE issuing deletes
persistStatusChange: no-op if the new status equals the current status
issues an UPDATE only when the state machine actually transitions

notes: BEST-EFFORT: failure to insert is SILENTLY ignored (Left _ -> pure ())
looks up item name from inventory snapshot; falls back to UUID-as-text if missing
emits PullRequestCreated only on successful insert
consumes: Service.Transaction.addItem (called after each successful item add)
downstream PullRequestCreated event is consumed by stockroom dashboard SSE

notes: thin wrapper: createSale then emit TransactionCreated (via saleToLegacyTransaction for the event payload)
no state machine guard; new sales are always valid

addItem.intent: Add an item to a sale with state-machine enforcement and inventory reservation
notes: ORDER: loadSale, state-guard, persistStatusChange, addSaleItem, emit TransactionItemAdded, createStockPull
if addSaleItem fails (inventory), the sale status was ALREADY persisted as InProgress
createStockPull failures do NOT roll back the item or status change
ItemNotFound -> err404; InsufficientInventory -> err400 with both numbers in body

spec.expected: Right item, status -> InProgress, ItemAdded + PullRequestCreated emitted

removeItem: looks up the item BEFORE deletion to capture sku and qty for the event
cancels pulls for this (txId, sku) AFTER the DB delete
emits PullRequestCancelled per pull that was actually cancelled (filtered by current vertex, excluding PullFulfilled and PullCancelled)
addPayment: state-machine guard runs against the stored status, not a recomputed one
does NOT finalize even if amount equals total; finalize is a separate explicit command
removePayment: looks up txId via getTxIdByPaymentId BEFORE deletion (post-delete the link is gone)

finalizeTx.intent: Transition InProgress -> Completed; decrement inventory; mark reservations Completed
notes: underlying TransactionDb.finalizeSale does inventory adjustment AND reservation completion
no rollback if emit fails (broadcaster publish is fire-and-forget)

spec.expected: Right sale{status=Completed}, inventory decremented, TransactionFinalized emitted

voidTx.intent: Void a sale (valid from Created, InProgress, or Completed); cancel any open stock pulls
notes: captures pulls list BEFORE void to know what to cancel
ORDER: state guard, getPullsByTransaction, voidSale (DB), emit TransactionVoided, cancelPullsForTransaction, emit PullRequestCancelled per non-terminal pull
GENERATES the refund UUID, per-item UUIDs, and per-payment UUIDs at the service layer (not the DB)
builds the typed Refund.RefundTransaction via toRefundTransaction; err500 if conversion fails
writes via writeRefund (which is DB.Transaction.Refund.writeTypedRefund under the hood)
original sale is marked is_refunded=true; refund tx points back via reference_transaction_id

guardRegEvent: translates InvalidRegCommand to err409 with reason in the body

openRegister.intent: Open a register via the RegisterMachine (valid only from RegClosed)

notes: computes variance = expectedDrawerAmount - countedCash on the loaded register
emits RegisterClosed with variance; variance can be negative (over) or positive (short)

loadPull: reconstructs SomePullState from PullVertex via fromVertex
guardPullEvent: translates InvalidPullCmd to err409 with reason in the body

notes: ORDER: load, run command, guard, updatePullStatus, emit PullStatusChanged
carries actorId from the command into the event for audit
if updatePullStatus fails it returns err500 with the DB error text
consumes: Service.Stock.acceptPull, startPulling, fulfillPull, reportIssue, retryPull (all dispatch through here)

fulfillPull.intent: Mark a pull as Fulfilled (valid only from PullPulling)

spec.expected: err409 Invalid command in Accepted state

notes: DOES NOT use transition: emits PullRequestCancelled (not PullStatusChanged) with the cancellation reason
valid from any non-terminal vertex; PullCancelled and PullFulfilled are absorbing

addMessage: loads the pull only to assert it exists; message can be added in any state
msgId is generated at the service layer via GenUUID

decisions.name: StateMachineEnforcedAtTypeLevel
decisions.what: PullState is GADT-indexed by PullVertex; transitions live in pullAction whose type uses crem ActionResult
why: The DSL prevents constructing a transition not in PullTopology.
Service.Stock.transition relies on this: it persists the new vertex without re-validating, trusting the machine output.
Illegal commands produce a PullEvent with constructor InvalidPullCmd, NOT a Haskell exception, so the service layer can map it to err409.

fromVertex: rebuilds typed state from a database-loaded PullVertex
lossless: every PullVertex maps to exactly one PullState constructor
toPullVertex: extracts the runtime tag from a SomePullState
used to persist the new state after a transition
pullAction: Pending  -> {Accepted via AcceptCmd, Cancelled via CancelCmd}; everything else is InvalidPullCmd
Accepted -> {Pulling via StartPullCmd, Cancelled via CancelCmd}
Pulling  -> {Fulfilled via FulfillCmd, Issue via ReportIssueCmd}; cannot be cancelled directly
Issue    -> {Accepted via RetryCmd, Cancelled via CancelCmd}
Fulfilled and Cancelled are absorbing; any further command yields InvalidPullCmd, no state change

notes: wraps pullAction; existential erasure means callers cannot inspect the new vertex's type
callers pattern-match on the returned PullEvent to know which transition fired
InvalidPullCmd indicates same-state with no transition; the SomePullState is unchanged
consumes: Service.Stock.transition (the only orchestrator that runs commands against this machine)

fromRegister: dispatches on registerIsOpen field to choose ClosedState or OpenState
regAction: Closed -> {Open via OpenRegCmd}; CloseRegCmd from Closed is InvalidRegCommand
Open   -> {Closed via CloseRegCmd}; OpenRegCmd from Open  is InvalidRegCommand
OpenRegCmd sets currentDrawer = expectedDrawer = startCash, sets openedBy
CloseRegCmd computes variance = expectedDrawer - countedCash, sets currentDrawer = countedCash

decisions.name: CremForLifecycleEnforcement
decisions.what: crem (singletons-based type-level state machines) for transaction, register, and stock-pull lifecycles
why: Transaction, register, and stock-pull state transitions are legally significant for cannabis compliance — an unintended Created -> Completed shortcut is a reportable violation.
crem encodes the topology in types via singletons + GADTs; any transition not declared in the Topology fails to compile.
All three machines pair (SomeXxxState, fromXxx, toSomeXxxState) so callers handle existential states without leaking type-level vertices.

fromTransaction: dispatches on transactionStatus field to choose the correct SomeTxState constructor
txAction: Created     -> {InProgress via AddItemCmd, Voided via VoidCmd}; everything else is InvalidTxCommand
InProgress  -> {InProgress via item/payment add+remove, Completed via Finalize, Voided via Void}
Completed   -> {Voided via Void, Refunded via Refund}; further item/payment ops are invalid
Voided and Refunded are absorbing; any further command yields InvalidTxCommand
AddItemCmd from Created sets transactionStatus = InProgress on the carried Transaction value, NOT just the type-level vertex

notes: callers pattern-match on TxEvent to know which transition fired
InvalidTxCommand carries a human-readable reason that Service.Transaction maps to err409 body
consumes: Service.Transaction.addItem, removeItem, addPayment, removePayment, finalizeTx, voidTx, refundTx

notes: extracts TransactionStatus from a SomeTxState by inspecting the singleton tag
consumes: Service.Transaction.persistStatusChange (detects when an actual transition happened)

cmdLabel: human-readable command names used in InvalidTxCommand error messages
MUST stay in sync with TxCommand constructors; partial functions would silently produce 'Cannot ?' messages

decisions.name: BoundedInMemoryReplay
decisions.what: TChan plus bounded Data.Sequence history per stream
why: Late SSE/WS subscribers need to catch up without scanning domain_events for every connect.
O(1) publish and O(history) replay.
Anything older than the bound is unreachable from this broadcaster; clients fall back to /admin/domain-events with a cursor for deeper history.
Bound is per-broadcaster from cfgLogBroadcastSize / cfgDomainBroadcastSize / cfgStockBroadcastSize / cfgAvailabilityBroadcastSize.

newBroadcaster: TChan is a broadcast channel; subscribers each get an independent dup
bMaxHistory is fixed at construction; cannot be resized at runtime
bNextSeq starts at 0; first published event has seq 0

publish.intent: Atomically bump seq, write to channel, append to bounded history
notes: ORDER inside the STM transaction: read seq, write seq+1, write to chan, trim history
history is dropped FIFO when length exceeds bMaxHistory (Seq.drop 1)

spec.input: pre <- currentSeq bc; publish bc evt; post <- currentSeq bc
replicateM_ (bMaxHistory + n) (publish bc evt); historyDepth bc

consumes: Katip.BroadcastScribe (every log line at or above the configured severity)

notes: subInitSeq is the seq at the moment of subscribe; events with that seq or later flow through subChan
subscribers DO NOT see history automatically; sseStream and wsHandler replay history themselves before reading subChan
no unsubscribe operation; channels are GC-cleaned when subscribers drop their reference
consumes: Infrastructure.AvailabilityRelay's own subscribe to the domain broadcaster

notes: returns all (seq, event) pairs with seq strictly greater than cursor
uses Seq.filter; O(n) over current history
result is bounded by bMaxHistory regardless of cursor value

currentSeq: next seq value that publish would assign (NOT the just-published one)
uses readTVarIO (no STM transaction); cheaper than subscribe for a snapshot

decisions.name: EventDrivenAvailabilityProjection
decisions.what: Availability is derived state, populated once from DB then maintained by streaming domain events
why: Avoids a second DB round-trip on every transaction-item event.
Cost is a startup race window where events between populateFromDb and subscribe are missed; acceptable because the relay only runs at app startup.
If the relay dies and is restarted, populateFromDb re-syncs from authoritative DB state.

runAvailabilityRelay.intent: Long-running thread translating DomainEvents into AvailabilityUpdates
notes: ORDER on startup: populateFromDb FIRST, then subscribe, then loop
events that arrive between populateFromDb and subscribe are lost (small race window)
loop is forever; on STM exception or DB error the thread dies and App.hs prints to stderr
no supervisor: relay death silently breaks the public feed until process restart
only InventoryEvt and TransactionItemAdded/Removed produce updates; other events are ignored

spec.expected: no AvailabilityUpdate published, sku removed from state

consumes: App.runWithEnv (forks this thread once at startup)
Server.Feed.wsHandler subscribes to the availability broadcaster this populates

populateFromDb: reservation totals aggregated by SKU via Map.fromListWith (+)
called only at relay startup; no periodic re-hydration; trusts the event stream after
if a reservation is mutated by someone bypassing the event emitter, the relay drifts permanently
updateAvailability: ItemDeleted clears both asItems AND asReserved entries for the sku; emits Nothing because the item is gone
TransactionItemRemoved decrements reservation; if it drops to 0 the entry is removed (Map.update with Nothing branch)
negative reservations are clamped to 0 by Map.update returning Nothing
publishFor reads asItems for the sku; if missing returns Nothing (no orphan updates)

decisions.name: AtprotoStyleSubscription
decisions.what: WebSocket subscription with monotonic seq cursor, modeled on com.atproto.sync.subscribeRepos
why: Public consumers (compliance reporting, third-party menu sites) need an audit-friendly event log, not snapshot polling.
The subscribe protocol is intentionally familiar to ATProto implementers.
Lexicons are served at /lexicons/app.cheeblr.* so consumers can introspect the schema.

decisions.name: SyntheticSnapshotFramesShareSeq
decisions.what: When a client connects without a cursor, every snapshot frame carries currentSeq
why: Snapshot frames are not in the broadcaster's history and have no real seq of their own.
Clients must tolerate non-strictly-monotonic seq across the snapshot block, then strictly monotonic from the first live event.
Documented in the lexicon, but easy to miss.

feedSubscribeApp: WAI Tagged Application that upgrades to WebSocket or 400s if the client did not request upgrade
fallback responds 400 with body 'WebSocket connection required'

notes: ORDER: acceptRequest, replay-or-snapshot, subscribe, withPingThread, loop
with cursor: replays Seq.filter (seq > cursor) from history, then live
without cursor: emits SYNTHETIC snapshot frames, all sharing currentSeq value, then live
snapshot frames are NOT in history and CANNOT be replayed by a re-subscribing client without cursor
ping thread is 30s
loop catches ConnectionException only; other exceptions kill the connection thread but not the server

spec.expected: frames where seq > 100, then live updates
snapshot of all current items at currentSeq, then live updates

feedStatusHandler: reads bHistory directly via readTVarIO; not a method on Broadcaster (slight encapsulation leak)
oldestSeq comes from listToMaybe on the history Seq; Nothing iff history is empty
feedSnapshotHandler: no pagination; full snapshot in one response
intended for clients that prefer one-shot polling over WS

notes: lexicon JSON is INLINED as Haskell ByteString literals
vendored deliberately to avoid a runtime asset path dependency; ugly but self-contained
404 for any path under /lexicons that is not one of the three known names

decisions.name: TraceIdViaWaiVault
decisions.what: Per-request TraceId stored in WAI Vault under a NOINLINE unsafePerformIO key
why: Servant handlers run in Handler with no convenient place to thread per-request context.
Vault is the canonical mechanism in the WAI ecosystem.
The same TraceId can be stamped on the outbound response and on every domain event written during the request, giving end-to-end traceability without changing handler signatures.
traceIdKey is created via unsafePerformIO with a NOINLINE pragma. This is the documented WAI pattern but it's a global mutable resource — only ever create one per process.

notes: process-global Vault key for stashing the per-request TraceId
NOINLINE pragma is REQUIRED; without it GHC may inline and create distinct Vault keys per call site
documented WAI pattern; do not duplicate this trick elsewhere without thinking it through

tracingMiddleware.intent: Ensure every request has a TraceId in the WAI vault and on the response
notes: ORDER: read X-Trace-Id header, parse OR generate fresh, insert in vault, run app, append response header
header parse: Types.Trace.parseTraceId requires a UUID-shaped string; malformed headers fall back to a fresh ID
client-supplied traces are TRUSTED; spoofing has no security impact (trace IDs are never used for authorization)
response header X-Trace-Id is APPENDED, not replaced; if a downstream layer set it, both will appear

spec.expected: fresh TraceId in vault, X-Trace-Id present on response
same TraceId in vault and response
fresh TraceId in vault, malformed value DISCARDED

consumes: App.runWithEnv (outermost middleware, runs on every request)

notes: Nothing if tracingMiddleware did not run before this handler (programmer error, not a runtime case)
callers should treat Nothing as 'tracing disabled' rather than failing the request
consumes: Effect.EventEmitter.runEventEmitterProd (stamps trace_id on every domain event)
Katip log enrichment (every log line gets the trace_id)
any handler that wants to surface trace_id in a response body or error
